because.text: Self-conducted Book I/GOV/Book III review at v0.6.6 (run-first: full suite re-run green before critique). TWO P0 found and fixed. P0-a warrant-go -- the third independent Book I evaluator -- truncated the R-S cost to uint32: 1+size(z) wraps at 2^32, making an unaffordable step look affordable and splitting warrant-go from the Python (bignum) and Rust (u64) impls; Book I s3.4 mandates ATP Exhausted for any step priced over 2^32-1. Fixed: cost in uint64, narrow only after the affordability check. P1 warrant-go carried a non-normative memory fence returning canonical DISSONANCE(ATP) on the size-1<=spent theorem -- if it ever fired (via the P0 wrap) it would launder an impl bug into a canonical outcome, which Book I s3.6 forbids; removed (Python/Rust have none). P0-b EVERY Ed25519 verifier in the federation (warrant PY+GO, sigma warrant_verify.py + anchor_governance.counted_sigs) accepted small-order/non-canonical public keys: an all-zero key lets an all-zero signature verify for ~17/64 messages under Python and ~16/64 under Go -- the mismatch is itself a consensus split, and without a keyring it binds a decision to any actor id. Fixed everywhere: reject the 8 canonical torsion encodings + non-canonical y>=p, byte/integer-only so all impls agree; roster keys unaffected, all real sigs still verify. P2 cross-repo co-sig skew documented (sigma insulated by the pinned 73758bdb Warrant SPEC snapshot). Fixes live in the warrant repo (d8350df) and here (tools). cmd@v1 check re-runs all Book suites + both differential harnesses + governance selftest + warrant_verify + anchors + Rust + warrant-go conformance; transcript pinned. Next: independent external audit of the ATP-boundary arithmetic and the Ed25519 blocklist completeness.

subject.note: Fable 5 self-review v0.6.6: two P0 fixed -- warrant-go Book I R-S uint32 overflow (consensus split) and small-order Ed25519 keys accepted by every verifier; merges 3 review tips. Suites green.
